root.description: Petdex is the public gallery of animated companions for Codex. Browse open-source companions, preview their animations, and install one with a single command.

header.browseDesc: Discover pets, creators, and curated collections
header.collectionsDesc: Featured character sets: South Park, Pokémon, more
header.creatorsDesc: Leaderboard of who is shipping the most pets
header.requestsDesc: Vote on pets the community wants curated next
header.vibesDesc: Cozy, edgy, mystical, heroic and more
header.buildDesc: Make and ship your own pixel pet
header.createDesc: Hatch a pet with Codex skill, 5 min from idea to sprite
header.submitDesc: Drop your pet folder so Petdex hosts it for everyone
header.docsDesc: Install the Petdex CLI and ship pets via npm
header.earnDesc: Manage campaigns, impressions, and click stats
header.advertiseDesc: Reach devs installing AI tools via sponsored cards
header.builtWithDesc: Apps the community is shipping on the Petdex format
header.communityDesc: Discord server with shipping channels and voice rooms
header.takedownDesc: Report a pet that uses your work
header.downloadDesc: macOS desktop app - your pet beside every agent

footer.rightsNotice: Pets are user-submitted fan art. Petdex does not claim rights to any underlying IP.

home.title: Petdex
home.tagline: The public gallery of animated companions for <brand />. Browse {totalPets}+ open-source companions, preview their states, and install one with a single command.
home.jsonLdDescription: Public gallery of animated companions for Codex. Install one with a single command.

metadata.title: Petdex Community
metadata.description: Join creators, collectors, and the Petdex team for featured creator spotlights, work-in-progress feedback, and weekly builds.

community.title: Build pixel pets in good company
community.description: The Petdex Community brings together creators, collectors, and the team behind the gallery. Get WIP feedback, follow weekly builds, and meet featured creators.

spotlight.title: Featured creators get the spotlight
spotlight.body: GRAYCRAFT, Scientific Minds, Crafter Originals. Every collection has a real creator behind it, and the community is where they take feedback and pick what ships next.

channels.title: Channels for shipping, not vibing only
channels.body: #wip for in-progress sprites, #cli-feedback for bug reports, #ideas synced with the public wishlist, and #ship-or-sink for weekend competitions.

roles.title: Roles you earn by playing
roles.body: @Creator unlocks when your first pet is approved. @Collector at three caught pets. @Featured Creator when your set lands as a curated collection.

voice.title: Voice rooms that are not dead
voice.body: Co-working for silent focus, pixel-jam for live sprite-art collabs, and office-hours with the Petdex team Fridays at 4pm GMT-5.

rules.title: Be kind, be specific, ship something

items.respect: No racism, sexism, or hate speech. Bans are immediate.
items.spam: No spam, no DM solicitation, no NFT shilling.
items.critique: Critique sprites, not creators. Honest, kind, specific.
items.languages: English, Spanish, and Chinese channels welcome. Pick yours.
items.ip: Pet IP belongs to the original creator. Credit always.
items.modlog: Petdex Team mod log is public in #bot-logs. No secrets.

suggestCollection.notePlaceholder: Optional: tell admins why this pet fits the collection

metadata.title: Advertise on Petdex
metadata.description: Reach developers who discover and install new AI and developer tools through Petdex sponsored cards.

hero.title: Put your dev tool in front of builders who try new things.
hero.body: Petdex has a growing audience of developers browsing Codex companions and developer culture. They already try CLI tools, AI workflows, and subscription products, making this a focused place to introduce AI and developer tools.

landing.pricingTitle: Buy viewable impressions without a sales call.
landing.finalTitle: Ready when your creative is ready.
landing.finalBody: Create a campaign, upload a clean sponsored card image, choose a prepaid package, and continue to Stripe checkout. You can edit the creative from your dashboard after launch.

checkout.successBody: Stripe is confirming the payment. Your campaign will start automatically once the webhook activates it.
checkout.cancelBody: No payment was captured. You can adjust the ad and try again.

audience.title: A developer-first audience
audience.body: Petdex visitors are developers and AI-tool users browsing a catalog built around Codex. They are comfortable installing tools and evaluating paid subscriptions.

placement.title: Subtle sponsored cards
placement.body: Ads appear as clearly labeled cards between pet cards, matching the gallery rhythm without interrupting browsing.

billing.title: Guaranteed viewable impressions
billing.body: You buy guaranteed viewable impressions. We also report hovers, clicks, CTR, and average time in view.

items.malware: No malware, phishing, scams, deceptive downloads, or credential collection.
items.adult: No adult, sexual, or explicit content.
items.hate: No hate, harassment, discriminatory, or abusive content.
items.illegal: No illegal products, regulated goods, or services that violate applicable law.
items.impersonation: No impersonation of people, companies, communities, or open-source projects.
items.misleading: No misleading AI claims, fake benchmarks, or ads that misrepresent what the tool does.

legal.title: Petdex can remove ads that do not fit the community.
legal.body: We reserve the right to pause, delete, or refuse any ad that does not align with Petdex policies, community expectations, or product quality standards. Removed ads are not reimbursed.

form.title: Ad title
form.creativeDisclosure: Your ad title, image, and description will appear in the sponsored card shown in the Petdex feed.
form.imageCta: Upload PNG, JPG, or WebP creative
form.imageHelp: Max 10 MB. Use a clean product image, logo card, or screenshot.
form.previewHelp: This is how your sponsored card will appear in the Petdex feed. Links are disabled while previewing.
form.previewFallbackDescription: A short description of your product will appear here.
form.pricingTrust: We only count viewable impressions: 50%+ of the ad visible for 2 seconds.
form.utmHelp: Optional. If left blank, Petdex adds utm_source=petdex to the ad link.
form.legalAccept: I accept that Petdex may pause or delete ads that do not align with its policies, and that those removals are not reimbursed.
form.signIn: Sign in to submit an ad

errors.imageTooLarge: Image must be 10 MB or smaller.

metadata.title: Create a Petdex ad campaign
metadata.description: Build a Petdex sponsored card, choose a prepaid impression package, and continue to Stripe checkout.

create.title: Create your sponsored card.
create.body: Add the creative, destination, tracking, and prepaid impression package for your Petdex campaign.

creative.title: Use a clear creative
creative.body: A logo card, product screenshot, or simple product visual works best in the Petdex feed.

review.title: Keep it community-safe
review.body: Ads remain subject to Petdex policies and can be removed if they mislead or harm the community.

billing.title: Checkout is prepaid
billing.body: Stripe collects payment after your campaign is created. Delivery starts after payment activation.

dashboard.removedFallback: This campaign was removed because it did not align with Petdex policies.

checkout.successBody: Stripe is confirming the payment. Your campaign will start automatically once the webhook activates it.

empty.title: Launch your first sponsored card

metadata.description: Update your Petdex sponsored card creative, destination, and tracking fields.

notEditable.title: This campaign cannot be edited

errors.imageTooLarge: Image must be 10 MB or smaller.

placeholders.bug: What broke? Include steps if you can.

success.title: Thanks. Got it.
success.body: Every note lands in the queue. We read them all.

errors.moreDetail: Tell us a bit more (4+ chars).

edits.title: Pending edits
edits.description: Owner-submitted text changes awaiting re-approval. The live page keeps showing the approved values until you act.
edits.noDiff: Edit submitted but no changes detected. Reject to clear.

campaigns.empty: No ad campaigns match these filters.

manifest.title: Manifest fetches
manifest.description: Who's pulling /api/manifest. IPs are SHA-256 hashed daily so they group within a day but can't be reversed.

telemetry.title: CLI usage telemetry
telemetry.description: Anonymous install volume, OS distribution, and agent adoption from petdex CLI users.

metadata.title: Submit a pet | Petdex
metadata.description: Upload and validate a Codex pet package for Petdex.

submit.title: Add your pet to Petdex
submit.body: Share a Codex-compatible animated pet package. Petdex checks the files locally, previews the sprite, and prepares the submission.
submit.createCta: Don't have a pet yet? Hatch one in Codex
submit.legalPrefix: By submitting, you confirm you have rights to the artwork or are creating fan content. Rights holders can request removal via our

drop.title: Upload a pet package
drop.instructions: Drop a folder or a ZIP with <petJson>pet.json</petJson> and <spritesheet>spritesheet.webp</spritesheet> (or .png). Recommended {width}×{height}, 8×9 frame grid.
drop.short: Drop a folder or a ZIP with pet.json + spritesheet.

check.title: Submission check
check.empty: Packages stay local until you confirm. Petdex checks the manifest and sprite dimensions before upload.

success.approved: {name} passed automated review and is live now.
success.rejected: {name} was rejected by automated review. You can adjust the package and submit it again.
success.hold: {name} needs manual review. We'll notify you when an admin decides.
success.review: {name} is in review. You'll be notified when it's approved.

details.duplicate: Reason: it appears to duplicate an existing pet. {summary}
details.duplicateFallback: The submitted assets matched an existing pet pack.
details.policy: Reason: automated review found possible policy issues, so an admin needs to review it before it can go live.
details.assets: Reason: automated review found an issue with the package files or spritesheet.
details.timeout: Reason: automated review took too long, so we held it for manual review instead of making a risky decision.
details.reviewFailed: Reason: automated review failed, so we held it for manual review instead of making a risky decision.
details.rejectedGeneric: Reason: automated review found a high-confidence issue with this submission.
details.holdGeneric: Reason: automated review was not confident enough to approve it automatically.

fallback.link: Open a GitHub issue with your assets
fallback.afterLink: and Hunter will upload it manually.

errors.rateLimited: Limit reached: 10 submissions per 24 hours. Try again tomorrow.
errors.missingField: The package is missing required submission data.
errors.invalidSpritesheet: The spritesheet is too small or has invalid dimensions.
errors.invalidAssetUrl: The uploaded assets did not pass the hosting check.
errors.invalidSlug: Pet id could not become a valid URL slug.
errors.invalidJson: The submitted JSON could not be read.

issues.dropPetFolderOrZip: Drop a pet folder or a .zip file.
issues.allPetsBundle: This looks like the all-pets bundle (petdex-approved.zip). Submit a single pet folder or its individual zip instead.
issues.zipMissingSpritesheet: Missing spritesheet.webp (or .png) at zip root. Found: {present}
issues.tooSmall: Spritesheet seems too small ({width}×{height}). Use an 8×9 frame grid (recommended {recommendedWidth}×{recommendedHeight}).

metadata.title: Pet requests
metadata.description: Vote on pets the community wants to see in Petdex. Most-upvoted requests get prioritized.

requests.title: Pets people are asking for
requests.body: When someone searches for a pet that doesn't exist yet, they can request it. Upvote the ones you'd want too. Most-upvoted go to the top of the curation list.

form.title: Request a pet
form.body: Describe the pet you'd like to see: character, theme, vibe. If someone else already asked for the same thing, your submission turns into an upvote on theirs.
form.bodyPet: Describe the pet you'd like to see: character, theme, vibe. If someone else already asked for the same thing, your submission turns into an upvote on theirs.
form.bodyCollection: Describe the set you want: IP, brand, cast, theme, and what pets should belong together.

type.petBody: One companion, character, object or creature.
type.collectionBody: A collectible set like Pixar, Star Wars or GRAYCRAFT.

form.petHint: Use Pet for one specific companion: a character, creature, object or desk buddy.
form.collectionHint: Use Collection for sets like Pixar, Star Wars, Pokémon starters or GRAYCRAFT: brand/IP name, pets that belong together and the official site.
form.placeholder: e.g. a Studio Ghibli chinchilla, a sleepy axolotl coder, Spider-Pig
form.placeholderPet: e.g. a Studio Ghibli chinchilla, a sleepy axolotl coder, Spider-Pig
form.placeholderCollection: e.g. Pixar desk buddies, Star Wars droids, Pokémon starters

metadata.title: Create a pet | Petdex
metadata.description: Hatch your own Codex pet in 5 minutes, then share it on Petdex.

create.title: Hatch a pet in Codex
create.body: Codex ships a built-in skill called Hatch Pet that generates a fully animated companion (9 states, 1536×1872) from a short description. Five minutes from idea to a sprite that lives in your app.

install.title: Get the Hatch Pet skill
install.body: Open Codex → Skills in the top navbar → find Hatch Pet → install.

hatch.title: Hatch your pet
hatch.afterCommand: . Describe the pet you want (an idea, a vibe, a thing on your desk). The skill generates the spritesheet and animations automatically.
hatch.tip: Tip: specifics travel further. "A tiny sock-elf with green eyes" beats "a creature".

activate.title: Activate it
activate.tipAfter: afterwards to wake or tuck it away.

share.title: Share it on Petdex
share.beforeLink: . Drag that folder (or its zip) to
share.afterLink: and Petdex hosts it for everyone to install with one curl.

twist.label: Twist a job, role, or object.
twist.body: Paperclip assistant, sock elf, microwave companion. The unexpected sticks.

vibe.label: One vibe.
vibe.body: Cozy, focused, mischievous, heroic. Don't try to be all of them.

ip.label: Skip clear IP.
ip.body: Fan-art is welcome but expect takedowns. Original concepts age better.

submit.title: Already have a pet?
submit.body: Drop the folder or zip and we'll publish it.

request.title: Burning through Codex tokens?
request.body: Drop a wish in the request queue. The community (and we) will pick it up.

creator.title: Web pet creator
creator.body: A browser-native studio so you can sketch, animate, and preview your pet without ever leaving Petdex. No Codex tokens required. Sign in to get notified when it lands.

metadata.description: {total} open-source projects building on top of Petdex: desktop companions, wellness apps, wearables, SDKs, and pet creators.

builtWith.title: Apps the community is shipping on Petdex
builtWith.subtitle: {total} open-source projects pull from the Petdex gallery, API, or pet format. Wellness apps, wearables, SDKs, and creators. Curated, evidence-required, no vibes.

desktopCompanion.label: Desktop companions
desktopCompanion.description: Apps that put a Petdex pet on your desktop.

wellness.label: Wellness & focus
wellness.description: Pomodoros, breaks, and focus tools that use Petdex pets as their on-screen character.

wearable.label: Wearable
wearable.description: Petdex pets running on Apple Watch, Garmin, and other wearables.

sdk.label: SDKs & runtimes
sdk.description: Libraries and runtimes that wrap the Petdex API or pet format for other platforms.

bundled.label: Bundled in larger apps
bundled.description: Petdex pets shipped as a feature of bigger products.

petCreator.label: Pet creators
petCreator.description: Solo creators distributing original pets in Petdex package format.

submit.title: Ship something with Petdex, then submit
submit.step1: Build something that pulls from the Petdex gallery, API, or pet format with explicit attribution.
submit.step2: Open a GitHub issue using the "Add my project to /built-with" template.
submit.step3: Provide an evidence quote (README link or code path proving the integration).
submit.step4: We review, take a 1280x720 screenshot, and merge.

metadata.title: Petdex brand kit
metadata.description: Download Petdex logos, icons, color tokens, and usage notes for press, projects, and community posts.

hero.title: Use Petdex clearly and consistently.
hero.body: Download official marks for launch posts, app integrations, community projects, and docs that reference Petdex.

assets.title: Logo files ready for real use
assets.body: Use SVG assets for web and print. Use the PNG app icon when you need the desktop product mark or a square social preview.

mark.title: Petdex mark
mark.description: The primary symbol for compact layouts, avatars, favicons, and badges.

wordmark.title: Petdex wordmark light
wordmark.description: Use on light or warm neutral backgrounds when the name needs to be readable without surrounding context.

wordmarkDark.title: Petdex wordmark dark
wordmarkDark.description: Use on dark backgrounds, screenshots, and launch graphics where the black wordmark loses contrast.

desktopIcon.title: Desktop icon
desktopIcon.description: Rounded app icon for Petdex Desktop references, press cards, and launch graphics.

do.title: Do
do.item1: Keep enough clear space around the mark.
do.item2: Use the original files without redrawing the shapes.
do.item3: Pair Petdex with clear attribution when featuring community pets.

dont.title: Don't
dont.item1: Stretch, rotate, skew, or recolor the logo arbitrarily.
dont.item2: Place the mark on low-contrast or visually noisy backgrounds.
dont.item3: Use Petdex branding to imply official approval of unrelated products.

system.title: Colors and type
system.body: Petdex uses cool app surfaces, purple-blue brand accents, and Geist type to feel like a useful tool with a tiny bit of creature energy.

metadata.title: About Petdex: Animated companions for Codex
metadata.description: Petdex is an open-source gallery of animated companions for Codex. Browse the catalog, install with one command, or submit your own.
metadata.ogDescription: An open-source gallery of animated companions for Codex.

hero.title: Petdex is the index of Codex pets
hero.body: An open-source gallery of {totalPets}+ animated companions for Codex. Install one with a single command and ship with company.

what.title: What Petdex is
what.body1: Petdex is a public, open-source registry. Every entry is a pet pack: a tiny bundle of metadata and pixel art that Codex knows how to display while you work. Think of it as a Pokédex for app companions.

body2.beforeCozy: The platform takes the guesswork out: there's a CLI for installing and submitting, a tagged gallery so finding the right companion doesn't require scrolling, and per-vibe collections like

pack.title: How a pet pack works
pack.betweenFiles: defines the metadata (name, the 9 animation states, frame durations) and
pack.afterSpritesheet: holds the pixel art as a horizontal strip. Codex loads them at startup and swaps states based on what's happening in your session.

whatIs.a: A Codex pet is a small animated pixel companion for the app. Each pet ships as a single pet.json describing 9 states (idle, working, sleeping, etc.) plus a horizontal spritesheet. Codex picks the right state automatically based on what you're doing in your session.

install.q: How do I install a pet?
install.a: From the gallery page or any pet detail page, copy the install command (looks like `npx petdex install boba`) and paste it into your terminal. The CLI fetches the pet from R2, drops it under ~/.codex/pets/<slug>/, and tells Codex to use it. Works on macOS, Linux, and Windows (via PowerShell).

origin.q: Where do the pets come from?
origin.a: Most pets are submitted by the community through the website or the petdex CLI. A handful are featured curated entries hand-picked from Crafter Station. Every pet is reviewed by an admin before it goes live in the gallery.

submit.q: Can I submit my own pet?
submit.a: Yes. Sign in with the petdex CLI (npx petdex login) and run `npx petdex submit ./my-pet/`. The CLI uploads the assets to R2 and queues the submission for review. You can also submit through the website at /submit. All you need is a pet.json file and a 9-state spritesheet.

openSource.q: Are Petdex pets free and open source?
openSource.a: Every pet on Petdex is free to install and use. Pets keep credit to their original authors. The Petdex platform itself (CLI + site) is open source under the Crafter Station umbrella.

taxonomy.q: What's a vibe? What's a kind?
taxonomy.a: Each pet is tagged with a kind (creature, object, character) and one to three vibes (cozy, playful, focused, mystical, …). These power the gallery filters and the per-vibe / per-kind landing pages so you can find a companion that matches your mood without scrolling 70+ entries.

money.a: It doesn't. Petdex is a community project run by Crafter Station. Storage and DB are absorbed by the platform team. No ads, no upsells, no telemetry beyond standard analytics on which pets get installed.

metadata.title: My submissions
metadata.description: Track the status of every pet you've sent to Petdex, customize your profile, and edit live entries.

edit.confirmWithdraw: Withdraw this edit? Current approved values stay live.
edit.submittedSuffix: . The page below shows the live approved version until Hunter signs off.
edit.modalBody: Text changes may auto-approve if they pass all criteria. Asset swaps always go to manual review.
edit.tagsHelp: Comma- or space-separated. Lowercase, max 8, hyphens ok.
edit.spritesheetHelp: Replaces the current sprite. Goes to manual review.
edit.metaHelp: Replaces the current metadata. Goes to manual review.

macHelp.pasteCommand: Paste the command above and press Return. If macOS asks for permission, allow it.

steps.insideCodex: inside Codex to wake or tuck it away.

profileShare.shareText: Check out {name}'s Codex pet collection on Petdex

takedownDialog.title: Report a pet
takedownDialog.body: Petdex hosts user-submitted fan-art pixel pets. We don't claim rights to the underlying IP of any character a community member chose to interpret. If you're a rights holder or authorized representative and want a pet removed, the fastest path is a GitHub issue.
takedownDialog.step1: Open a takedown request with the button below.
takedownDialog.step2: Tell us which pet and your relationship to the IP.
takedownDialog.step3: We review manually. Typical response under 48 hours. Filings stay public on GitHub for transparency.
takedownDialog.step4: Approved takedowns remove the pet from the gallery and installer endpoints.
takedownDialog.nonIp: Non-IP issues such as broken sprites or wrong descriptions should use a regular issue instead.
takedownDialog.confirmation: By submitting to Petdex you confirm you have rights to the artwork or are creating fan content. Petdex acts on good-faith takedown requests.
